concerns.text: Task T013 (generate_llm_samples.py) fails to implement Constitution Principle VI (Code Generation Transparency). It mandates logging 'model_id, prompt_hash, generation_seed' but omits the 'exact prompt' and 'API endpoint' required by the Constitution. This is a silent relaxation of the constitutional constraint for LLM traceability.

Task T012 (fetch_human_samples.py) defines metadata sidecars with 'issue_id' but does not explicitly mandate logging the 'Issue/PR URL' as required by Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) and Plan.md. Relying on an ID without the URL violates the 'Verified Accuracy' constraint which demands traceability to the primary source.

Task T014 introduces a specific numeric threshold ('≥95% validity') not present in Spec FR-003. While FR-003 only requires logging/excluding failures, T014 adds a hard stop condition ('flag for re-collection') that effectively narrows the acceptance criteria and introduces a new constraint not authorized by the spec, risking scope creep or pipeline failure if the spec's intent was merely exclusion.

Task T027 (compare_distributions.py) implements the 'Blocked Permutation Test' but omits the explicit instruction to apply 'Bonferroni correction'. Spec FR-006 and Section 4.2 explicitly mandate this correction for the family-wise error rate. Omitting this step in the task description creates a risk that the deliverable will fail to satisfy the FR, constituting a semantic weakening of the statistical requirement.
